title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — July 1, 2026
headline: China's Ethnic Unity Law entered force July 1; California scores 87.7 in first benchmark assessment.
summary: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1. The law codifies existing assimilation policy, activates Article 63 extraterritorial criminal liability for diaspora members, and coincides with the dissolution of Hong Kong's last pro-democracy party. Both Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch describe the law as embedding practices already documented and priced in China's score of 19.5 of 100. The resulting marginal harm delta is approximately 1.5 composite points — below the 5-point change threshold. China holds at 19.5 of 100 in the Critical band. A forward trigger is now live: the first documented Article 63 enforcement action is the next scored threshold event. California received its first formal benchmark score of 87.7 of 100 — the Exemplary band, ranked 5th in the United States Index. The Housing and Homelessness Agency and a companion-chatbot safety law both took effect July 1. The Democratic Republic of Congo's Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak continues at 1,307-plus confirmed cases and 377-plus deaths across four provinces. France and Germany both recorded imported cases; a United States evacuation is underway. DRC holds at 2.3 of 100. Venezuela's earthquake death toll rose to 1,943 on Day 7; 43,200 remain missing; the 14-day assessment checkpoint is July 8. Russia's floor at 0 of 100 is reinforced by deliberate strikes on city centers and energy infrastructure — the 2026 civilian-casualty rate is running 20 percent above 2025. Zero score changes were proposed. This is the tenth consecutive cycle with an empty proposal queue. Fourteen false-positive screening records were documented.

topSignals.title: China 19.5 of 100: Ethnic Unity and Progress Law in Force July 1 — Article 63 Diaspora Liability Active; Hong Kong's Last Pro-Democracy Party Dissolved
topSignals.whyItMatters: China's Ethnic Unity Law took effect July 1, codifying assimilation policy and making diaspora members criminally liable for speech deemed to threaten national unity. The score holds at 19.5. The first enforcement action is now the forward trigger.
topSignals.description: China holds at 19.5 of 100 in the Critical band. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1 — the effective date written into the statute. The law codifies assimilation policy, mandates ethnic-linguistic conformity, and establishes Article 63: a provision imposing extraterritorial criminal liability on diaspora members for conduct deemed to threaten national unity outside China's borders. On the same day, the Democratic Alliance for the Betterment and Progress of Hong Kong — the last pro-democracy party operating in Hong Kong — announced its dissolution. Both Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch characterize the law as codifying practices already documented and already priced in China's score. The phrase Amnesty uses is 'existing practices now embedded in national law.' The law is genuine in-force own-conduct — it is not pre-adjudication. But the marginal realized-harm delta is two quarter-steps: Equity (EQU) from 1.5 to 1.25 and Empathy (EMP) from 2.0 to 1.75. That yields approximately 1.5 composite points — below the 5-point change threshold. China is confirmed at 19.5 of 100; confidence: high. China sits 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing band boundary at 20.0 — the same line it crossed in May 2026. A forward trigger is now active: the first documented Article 63 enforcement action — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member tied explicitly to the new law — constitutes new realized harm, scorable toward further Equity and Boundaries docks. That trigger did not materialize on day one. The Hong Kong party dissolution adds Boundaries (BND) pressure consistent with an already-priced pattern of civil-society suppression.

evidence.claim: Amnesty International July 2026: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1, codifying assimilation policy — described by Amnesty as embedding 'existing practices now embedded in national law'; Article 63 imposes extraterritorial criminal liability on diaspora members for conduct deemed to threaten national unity.

Human Rights Watch July 2026: The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law codifies existing repression — including language assimilation mandates and transnational surveillance infrastructure — rather than introducing new categories of conduct; HRW urges foreign governments to designate Article 63 enforcement as a sanctions trigger.

topSignals.title: California 87.7 of 100: First Formal Benchmark Assessment — Exemplary Band, Rank 5; Housing Agency and Chatbot Safety Law Both Effective July 1
topSignals.whyItMatters: California has never been formally assessed in this benchmark. Its first score of 87.7 of 100 puts it in the Exemplary band at rank 5 in the United States Index. Three new policies took effect July 1. The integration premium of 8 points confirms consistent strength across all eight scoring dimensions.
topSignals.description: California receives its first formal benchmark score of 87.7 of 100 — the Exemplary band, ranked 5th in the United States Index. This is the first formal assessment of any major US state in the July cycle series. The composite reconstructs cleanly: base score 79.7 plus an integration premium of 8.0. The integration premium — which can add up to 10 points — is awarded for consistent high performance across all eight benchmark dimensions with near-zero inter-dimension variance. California meets that bar. Three policy-level changes took effect July 1 simultaneously with this assessment. First, the new Housing and Homelessness Agency — the first state agency dedicated entirely to the two issues — launched under a July 1 executive order. Second, Senate Bill 243, which mandates safety disclosures and age verification for companion-chatbot platforms, became law. Third, automated-decision-system anti-discrimination rules from the California Department of Fair Employment and Housing took effect. These are positive own-conduct signals consistent with the 87.7 score but do not independently clear the 5-point change threshold from the starting point. Tempering factors: California has 161,548 homeless residents — 27.9 percent of the national total, though the year-on-year figure is down 4 percent. Protest-policing incidents in Los Angeles in June (tear gas and batons used at peaceful demonstrations) are logged as an Accountability (ACC) watch flag. Neither the homelessness caseload nor the protest-policing incident independently clears the downgrade threshold. California is confirmed at 87.7 of 100; confidence: medium. Watch flags: Accountability (protest policing pattern), Equity (structural homelessness despite new agency), Action (Housing Agency performance verification needed).

evidence.claim: California Governor's Office July 1, 2026: Governor Newsom signed the executive order launching the Housing and Homelessness Agency — the first California state agency dedicated solely to housing affordability and homelessness reduction — effective July 1, 2026.

HUD Annual Homeless Assessment Report 2026: California has 161,548 homeless residents — 27.9 percent of the national total; the year-on-year count is down 4 percent from 2025, the first decline since 2020; four counties continue to show chronic unsheltered populations exceeding pre-pandemic levels.
evidence.source: US Department of Housing and Urban Development

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 of 100: Ebola Outbreak Internationally Active — France and Germany Imported Cases; US Evacuation Underway; July Peak Window Active
topSignals.whyItMatters: The DRC Ebola outbreak spread internationally: France recorded an imported case June 24 and Germany followed. The US is evacuating exposed personnel. The outbreak spans four provinces and 15 million people. DRC's score holds at 2.3 of 100. The floor-crossing trigger has not occurred.
topSignals.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo holds at 2.3 of 100 — the 18th consecutive emergency-cycle assessment. The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak now stands at 1,307-plus confirmed cases and 377-plus deaths across four provinces in DRC's northeast, with the fourth province — Haut-Uele — added June 30. The WHO Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) has been active since May 16. The outbreak's international spread is confirmed: France recorded the first EU-imported case on June 24 — a humanitarian worker — and Germany recorded a second imported case shortly after. The United States has initiated evacuation procedures for exposed personnel. The ECDC describes this as the fastest-growing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak on record. No approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. Contact tracing runs at approximately 45 percent — about half the rate needed to stop transmission. Community mistrust is impeding safe burials. The international spread (France, Germany, US evacuation) does not constitute DRC government own-conduct. It is epidemiological progression driven by travel and humanitarian-worker exposure. The scorable element is the DRC government's own response capacity, which remains weak. M23's eastern mass-killing harm is attributed to Rwanda, not DRC. The floor-crossing trigger — documented deliberate government obstruction of WHO response corridors — has not occurred. July is the natural seasonal Ebola peak window for this strain. DRC is confirmed at 2.3 of 100; confidence: high.

evidence.claim: WHO July 2026: Ebola outbreak in DRC at 1,307-plus confirmed cases and 377-plus deaths; four provinces affected in DRC's northeast including Haut-Uele (added June 30); Bundibugyo strain; PHEIC active since May 16; no approved vaccine; contact tracing at approximately 45 percent; 19 cases in Uganda.

ECDC July 2026: France recorded the first EU-imported Ebola case on June 24 — a humanitarian worker returning from DRC; Germany recorded a second imported case; US evacuation of exposed personnel is underway; outbreak described as the fastest-growing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak on record; Haut-Uele province borders South Sudan and the Central African Republic.

topSignals.title: Venezuela 18.0 of 100: Day 7 Earthquake Response — Death Toll 1,943; 43,200 Missing; July 8 Checkpoint One Week Out
topSignals.whyItMatters: Venezuela's earthquake death toll rose to 1,943 on Day 7 of the June 24 response. The missing-persons count stands at 43,200. The 14-day assessment checkpoint — the standard window for evaluating whether response failures reflect capacity or deliberate obstruction — is July 8, one week away. Score holds at 18.0 of 100.
topSignals.description: Venezuela holds at 18.0 of 100. Day 7 of the response to the June 24 twin earthquakes (magnitude 7.2 and 7.5). The confirmed death toll rose to 1,943 — up 224 from 1,719 on June 30. Reported missing persons stand at 43,200, revised downward from 46,600 on June 30 — a count reconciliation consistent with shifting from preliminary field estimates to more systematic records. This revision is not interpreted as 'people found.' The July 8 date — the 14-day mark from the June 24 earthquake — is now one week out. The two-week mark is the standard assessment window for distinguishing government capacity failure from deliberate obstruction or diversion. Aid acceptance from international partners continues: UNICEF completed a second 48-tonne supply shipment on June 30 and the United States has committed 300 million dollars under OFAC General License 60. These are donor actions, not Venezuelan own-conduct. The scored question remains what Venezuela's own government does to protect and assist its people. Domestic relief remains slow, disjointed, and access-restricted — approximately 14,000 troops and police remain deployed. The downgrade trigger — documented deliberate aid obstruction or diversion, or suppression of casualty data — has not materialized. The upgrade trigger — demonstrably effective, transparent, and sustained reconstruction — has not materialized. Both directions remain blocked; the net is sub-threshold. Venezuela is confirmed at 18.0 of 100; confidence: medium. Watch flags: Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) remain open through July 8.

evidence.claim: OCHA Venezuela July 1, 2026: death toll rose to 1,943 as of Day 7; 43,200 still reported missing following preliminary-to-systematic count reconciliation; approximately 14,000 troops and police deployed in affected areas; international aid acceptance continuing but domestic distribution remains slow and access-restricted.

US State Department June 25, 2026: OFAC General License 60 authorizes Venezuela earthquake relief transactions through October 23, 2026; commitment doubled to 300 million dollars through UN and faith-based channels.

topSignals.title: Russia and Israel 0 of 100: Floors Reinforced — 2026 Russia Casualty Rate 20 Percent Above 2025; Tenth Consecutive Zero-Change Cycle
topSignals.whyItMatters: Russia's floor at 0 of 100 is reinforced by deliberate strikes on city centers and energy infrastructure — the 2026 civilian-casualty rate is running 20 percent above the same period in 2025. Israel's floor is reinforced by continued Lebanon strikes and Gaza aid restrictions. This is the tenth consecutive cycle with zero score proposals.
topSignals.description: Russia holds at 0 of 100. Russia enters the priority cycle on July 1, replacing Sudan, South Sudan, Somalia, and Yemen from June 30. Deliberate targeting of city centers and energy infrastructure is documented: 22-plus civilians killed in a single night's strikes during the assessment window. The OHCHR's total documented casualties in Ukraine have reached 62,716. The 2026 casualty rate is running 20 percent higher than the comparable period in 2025. Continuation of an already-priced pattern — no lower score exists. Russia is confirmed at 0 of 100; confidence: high. Israel holds at 0 of 100. Lebanon strikes continue from the pattern established June 30 (83 killed, 141 wounded from 100-plus airstrikes). Gaza conditions remain: 77 percent of Gaza's population faces acute food insecurity; 37 humanitarian organizations have been banned; 992-plus people killed since the most recent ceasefire. The UN Commission of Inquiry genocide finding (report A/HRC/62/CRP.2, June 23) remains on the record. No withdrawal or restored humanitarian access constitutes positive own-conduct. Israel is confirmed at 0 of 100; confidence: high. The tenth consecutive zero-score-change cycle extends the record. The threshold events that would generate proposals — China Article 63 enforcement, DRC Ebola floor-crossing, Sudan ceasefire, Venezuela aid obstruction documentation, UnitedHealth Group adjudicated finding, Somalia formal famine declaration — remain unresolved. Fourteen false-positive screening records were documented tonight — matching the all-time series high set June 30.

evidence.claim: OHCHR Ukraine Monitoring Mission July 2026: total documented civilian casualties in Ukraine reach 62,716; the 2026 casualty rate is running 20 percent higher than the comparable period in 2025; continued deliberate targeting of residential areas, city centers, and energy infrastructure documented.

OCHA OPT July 2026: 77 percent of Gaza population facing acute food insecurity; 37 humanitarian organizations banned from operating; 992-plus people killed since most recent ceasefire; Kerem Shalom crossing congested; aid-truck throughput collapsed from 4,200 to 590 trucks per week.

boundaryWatch.trigger: First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member — is the next scored threshold event. The law entered force July 1; enforcement has not yet occurred. A durable positive reversal in Hong Kong civil-society access would be the upgrade trigger toward 20.0.
boundaryWatch.note: China at 19.5 Critical sits 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. The Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1 — assimilation codified, Article 63 diaspora liability now active. First formal boundary-proximity watch since the May 2026 Critical band crossing.

boundaryWatch.trigger: EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 — 32 days away. The model ban/clearance/lift arc resolved net-neutral June 30. White House 30-day vetting framework is the next forward factor for Fable 5.
boundaryWatch.note: Anthropic sits 0.9 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. Eleventh consecutive cycle in this range. The Commerce Department export-control arc on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 resolved net-neutral June 30 — score unchanged at 59.1. EU AI Act applies August 2.

boundaryWatch.trigger: NLRB review of the unfair-labor-practice charge filed after Apple closed its Towson, Maryland store — its first unionized store. An NLRB complaint or ruling is the conversion trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Apple sits 0.6 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. The Congressional Labor Caucus call for an NLRB investigation remains pre-adjudication. Seventh boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A new ceasefire agreement AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanese territory AND reconstruction delivery together constitute scored positive own-conduct — the upgrade trigger from 17.2 toward 20.0. The June 28 Washington framework is closed.
boundaryWatch.note: Lebanon at 17.2 Critical sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Israeli strikes continue; the ceasefire framework collapsed; Hezbollah rejected the June 28 renewal. Lebanon is scored on its own conduct only — harm from Israeli strikes is attributed to Israel, not Lebanon.

boundaryWatch.trigger: 14-day checkpoint July 8 — documented aid obstruction or deliberate diversion is the downgrade trigger; sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger toward 20.0. Action and Accountability watch flags open.
boundaryWatch.note: Venezuela at 18.0 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. Day 7 earthquake response; death toll 1,943; 43,200 missing. Fourteen-day checkpoint is July 8. Second boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Realized WFP food-assistance cuts plus a formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Borno with documented aid obstruction — August IPC lean-season update is the next checkpoint.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria at 18.0 Critical sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno priced in the June 19 band crossing. WFP imminent-cuts warning is a forward trigger. First formal boundary-proximity watch entry.

boundaryWatch.trigger: July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window — active. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors in Haut-Uele, Ituri, or North Kivu is the floor-crossing trigger. International spread (France, Germany, US evacuation) does not affect trigger conditions.
boundaryWatch.note: DRC at 2.3 Critical through cycle 18 of the Ebola emergency. 1,307-plus cases and 377-plus deaths as of July 1; France and Germany imported cases confirmed; US evacuation underway. No approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. July is the natural Ebola peak window.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A distinct civilian mass-casualty event from Hormuz navigation — not yet in evidence — would be a new scored threshold event toward the floor. Documented de-escalation or release of detained protesters at scale would be the upgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Iran at 2.5 Critical sits 2.5 points above the floor. 784-plus executions year-to-date — a 37-year high. Strait of Hormuz central channel mined; transit fees demanded; hundreds of vessels stranded. Gulf strikes continue an already-priced arc. First formal floor-proximity watch entry.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bengali Muslim and Rohingya expulsions documented in June 2026; detect-delete-deport digital surveillance continuing. Not assessed this cycle.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 15.6 Critical sits 0.6 points above the Critical floor through a seventeenth consecutive monitoring cycle. Not assessed tonight.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration covering Buur Hakaba or Baidoa combined with documented government aid obstruction is the scored change trigger. End-of-June declaration window passed. Famine risk window extends through September 2026.
boundaryWatch.note: Somalia at 4.7 Critical. Buur Hakaba at IPC AMN Phase 5 with GAM rate 37.1 percent. First priority assessment completed June 30. Not assessed tonight. Eighth floor-proximity cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity downgrade trigger. Durable verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — upgrade trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Kuwait at 25.0 Developing sits 5.0 points above the Critical boundary. EQU watch flag open on bidoon and migrant-worker emergency-protection performance under active Iran war conditions. Third boundary-watch cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review. Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) was reassessed in June on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Mali's starting-point score of 12.5 is above the likely calibrated range.
boundaryWatch.note: Mali at 12.5 Critical on a starting-point seed score. The Sahel calibration flag is in its eighth consecutive day. The gap with Burkina Faso (6.3, reweighted June 1 on active-state-perpetration conduct) persists. Coordinator review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. No timeline set.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DOJ False Claims Act complaint (kickbacks and disability discrimination) and Barrows v. Humana class action (nH Predict AI claim denials). Either concluded finding confirms the Developing-band designation.
boundaryWatch.note: Humana at 35.2 Developing, 4.8 points below the Functional boundary. Tenth cycle post-downgrade. Not assessed this cycle. Neither the DOJ complaint nor the Barrows class action has a fixed hearing date.

emergingRisks.risk: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1, activating Article 63 extraterritorial criminal liability for diaspora members. Governments with large Chinese diaspora populations — including Australia, Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United States — are the observable proxies for first Article 63 enforcement.
emergingRisks.detail: Article 63 of the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law criminalizes diaspora conduct deemed to threaten national unity, including speech, assembly, and advocacy. The first documented enforcement action — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member tied explicitly to Article 63 — would constitute new realized harm scorable toward further Equity (EQU) and Boundaries (BND) docks for China. China at 19.5 is 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. Enforcement pressure adds downward force; durable positive reversal in HK civil society access would be the counter-trigger.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Ebola now internationally active with confirmed imported cases in France and Germany and a US evacuation underway. The July natural Ebola peak window is active. The cross-border frontier with South Sudan (0 of 100) established June 30 continues to be the highest-urgency compounding risk.
emergingRisks.detail: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak reached 1,307-plus cases and 377-plus deaths as of July 1. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent. No approved vaccine. France and Germany imported cases confirm the outbreak is now crossing borders. The US evacuation signals that government humanitarian-worker exposure has occurred. If the outbreak crosses into South Sudan — which is at 0 of 100 for separate crises — and South Sudan's own response proves inadequate, that government response failure would constitute scorable own-conduct. The July peak window makes this the highest-priority forward risk in the series.

emergingRisks.risk: Venezuela's earthquake response reaches the 14-day checkpoint on July 8 — one week away. The death toll is rising steadily to 1,943. The missing-persons figure stands at 43,200. Domestic access remains restricted with approximately 14,000 troops and police deployed.
emergingRisks.detail: The two-week mark is the standard window for distinguishing government capacity failure from deliberate aid obstruction or diversion. If documented deliberate obstruction, diversion of relief supplies, or suppression of casualty data occurs before July 8, the Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags reach threshold — downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. A demonstrably effective, transparent, and sustained reconstruction effort is the upgrade trigger. Venezuela is 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0.

emergingRisks.risk: UnitedHealth Group's July 29 Q2 earnings call is the next material information event. DOJ criminal and civil probes widened to Optum Rx physician reimbursement and MA-inflation billing. All remain pre-adjudication.
emergingRisks.detail: Any material DOJ disclosure, investor-facing admission, indictment, or settlement at or around the July 29 earnings call would be scored under the Accountability (ACC) and Integrity (INT) dimensions. The near-floor score of 10.2 already prices the Senate 'aggressively gaming' finding and prior probes. A concluded adverse finding would constitute a new scored event.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's El Obeid siege, South Sudan's DRC Ebola cross-border exposure, and Somalia's famine declaration window — all from the June 30 assessment cluster — carry forward without new assessment tonight. Their forward triggers remain open.
emergingRisks.detail: Sudan at 0 of 100: El Obeid RSF drone siege is in its 19th-plus day; al-Burhan's ceasefire rejection stands. The RSF assault pattern killed roughly 6,000 in El-Fasher in three days in May 2025. South Sudan at 0 of 100: DRC Ebola spread to Haut-Uele creates the highest-priority new cross-border compounding risk. A realized incursion into South Sudan plus documented response failure is the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger. Somalia at 4.7: Buur Hakaba IPC AMN Phase 5 (GAM 37.1 percent); famine declaration window through September 2026.

emergingRisks.risk: Lebanon-Israel strikes continue without a new framework after the June 28 Washington ceasefire collapsed. The Lebanon-Israel conflict produced 83 deaths from 100-plus Israeli airstrikes on June 30.
emergingRisks.detail: The forward trigger for Lebanon is unchanged: a new ceasefire agreement combined with Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanon and reconstruction access. That process must restart from zero. Israel's floor at 0 of 100 is reinforced by continued Lebanon strikes and Gaza conditions. Lebanon sits 2.8 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary. The Boundaries (BND) and Action (ACT) dimensions remain the active watch dimensions for Lebanon.

emergingRisks.risk: Nigeria's August IPC lean-season update will determine whether WFP food-assistance cuts materialize and whether a formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration is issued in Borno. These are the scored triggers from the June 19 band crossing to 18.0 of 100.
emergingRisks.detail: Nigeria at 18.0 is 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. The IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno is already priced. A formal Phase 5 famine declaration with documented government aid obstruction, or realized WFP assistance cuts, constitutes the scored threshold event. The August lean-season peak will compound the situation.

emergingRisks.risk: The EU AI Act becomes fully applicable on August 2 — 32 days from July 1. General-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. xAI and OpenAI have not published compliance roadmaps.
emergingRisks.detail: A post-applicability non-compliance finding for general-purpose AI models constitutes a Tier-5 methodological finding and would move scores. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. OpenAI's US-government-mandated restricted GPT-5.6 release (June 29) is US government conduct, not OpenAI own-conduct; logged for next rotation. The compliance gap between labs will become legally significant on August 2.

emergingRisks.risk: The Sahel calibration flag is in its eighth consecutive day. Mali holds a starting-point seed score of 12.5 of 100 compared with Burkina Faso's reweighted 6.3 of 100 on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. No coordinator review timeline is set.
emergingRisks.detail: The coordinator-level review covers Mali, Niger, and potentially Chad. Mali's enforced-disappearance and extrajudicial-killing pattern — documented by UN Special Procedures as potentially constituting crimes against humanity — remains unresolved in the calibration framework. The gap has persisted through the entire June/July series without narrowing.

emergingRisks.risk: Yemen's Houthi aid obstruction continues: WFP's termination of all 365 northern-area staff contracts remains in effect; 73 UN workers detained, one died in custody. Doha talks are the only forward trigger and have no confirmed timeline.
emergingRisks.detail: A durable reversal of the forced WFP staff terminations and release of all 73 detained UN workers would constitute scored positive own-conduct for Yemen — the upgrade trigger from 0 of 100. Without food-agency presence, delivery to 22.3 million people in need is severely constrained. Whether Doha talks produce a humanitarian corridor for Yemen is the observable forward question.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — China Codified-Policy Attribution (Ethnic Unity Law in Force July 1)
sectorAlerts.headline: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1 — the first codified-policy attribution case in the July series; the law embeds existing practices in statute; the first Article 63 enforcement action is the next scored event.
observations: The law codifies assimilation, linguistic conformity, and ethnic-identity suppression in statutory form. Article 63 is the new element: extraterritorial criminal liability for diaspora members deemed to threaten national unity. This is the first such provision on the China scorecard.

Both Amnesty International and HRW characterize the law as codifying existing documented practices — not introducing new categories of conduct. This triggers the 'already-priced' analytical test: the in-force date does not independently generate a new score dock if the harm the law authorizes was already scored.

China at 19.5 is 0.5 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. The Equity (EQU) and Empathy (EMP) dimensions are the active forward-watch dimensions. The Boundaries (BND) dimension is under additional pressure from the HK party dissolution.

The Hong Kong Democratic Alliance — the last pro-democracy party in HK — dissolved July 1, coinciding with the law's entry into force. This closes the last institutional civil-society channel for political opposition in HK. BND pressure is reinforced.

sectorAlerts.sector: US States — California First Baseline (Exemplary Confirmed at 87.7)
sectorAlerts.headline: California's first formal benchmark assessment at 87.7 Exemplary sets the anchoring reference for the United States Index — three positive policies effective July 1; integration premium confirmed; homelessness and protest policing are watch flags.
observations: California's 87.7 composite reconstructs as base score 79.7 plus integration premium 8.0. The integration premium is awarded for consistent high performance across all eight dimensions with near-zero inter-dimension variance. California meets the threshold for the full 8.0-point premium.

Three policy launches effective July 1: (1) Housing and Homelessness Agency — first state agency dedicated solely to housing and homelessness; (2) SB 243 companion-chatbot safety law — age verification and disclosure mandates for AI companion platforms; (3) ADS anti-discrimination rules — automated-decision-system equity requirements from the California DFEH.

Tempering factors: 161,548 homeless residents (27.9 percent of national total, though down 4 percent year-on-year). LA protest policing incidents in June — tear gas and batons at peaceful demonstrations — are logged as an Accountability (ACC) watch flag. Neither clears the 5-point threshold independently.

California (population 40 million) is the most populous US state assessed in this benchmark. Its Exemplary baseline is the highest score in the US States Index behind states that have not yet been formally assessed. The watch flags (ACC, EQU, ACT) are open for subsequent cycles.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Critical and Floor Cluster (Russia + Israel + DRC; July 1 Assessed Set)
sectorAlerts.headline: Russia and Israel hold at the absolute floor; DRC, Iran, and Burkina Faso hold near-floor; the cluster is stable and fully priced — threshold events remain unresolved for all five.
observations: Russia (0 of 100): 22-plus civilians killed in a single night's deliberate city-center and energy-infrastructure strikes; 2026 casualty rate running 20 percent above 2025; 62,716 total OHCHR documented casualties. Floor reinforced. No lower score possible.

Israel (0 of 100): Lebanon strikes continuing from the June 30 pattern; Gaza food insecurity at 77 percent; 37 humanitarian organizations banned; 992-plus killed since most recent ceasefire; UN COI genocide finding on the record. Floor reinforced.

DRC (2.3 of 100): Ebola internationally active — France and Germany imported cases; US evacuation underway; July peak window active; contact tracing at 45 percent; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine. The floor-crossing trigger — deliberate government obstruction of WHO corridors — has not occurred. 18th emergency cycle.

Iran (2.5 of 100): 784-plus executions year-to-date at a 37-year high; Hormuz central channel mined; Gulf strikes continue an already-priced arc. Near-floor reinforced. Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100): Sahel lean-season crisis; calibration flag (eighth day). Near-floor reinforced. Sudan, South Sudan, Somalia, and Yemen (all at or near floor from June 30) are not in tonight's priority cluster but carry forward at their confirmed scores.

sectorAlerts.headline: Day 7: toll at 1,943 dead and 43,200 missing; July 8 checkpoint is one week out; score holds at 18.0 of 100; both upgrade and downgrade triggers remain unmet.
observations: Venezuela (18.0 of 100): Death toll rose from 1,719 on June 30 to 1,943 on July 1 — a daily increment consistent with ongoing search-and-rescue recovery. Missing persons revised to 43,200 from 46,600 on June 30 — preliminary-to-systematic count reconciliation, not found persons.

Aid acceptance continues from international partners: UNICEF 48-tonne shipment arrived June 30; US commitment at 300 million dollars under OFAC General License 60. Domestic delivery remains slow, opaque, and access-restricted with approximately 14,000 security personnel deployed.

The July 8 checkpoint — the 14-day mark from the June 24 earthquake — is one week out. This is the standard assessment window for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction. Both the Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags remain open.

Venezuela at 18.0 sits 2.0 points below the Critical-to-Developing boundary at 20.0. The scored downgrade trigger remains documented deliberate aid obstruction or diversion. The scored upgrade trigger remains demonstrably effective, transparent, and sustained reconstruction.

sectorAlerts.sector: Technology — Anthropic Model-Ban Arc Resolved; EU AI Act 32 Days
sectorAlerts.headline: Anthropic's model ban/clearance/lift arc (June 12–30) resolved net-neutral; score confirmed at 59.1; EU AI Act applies August 2 — 32 days from July 1.
observations: Anthropic (59.1 Functional): The Commerce Department's June 12 ban on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for cyber-capability concerns, followed by June 26–27 clearance and June 30 full lift, resolved net-neutral. The ban was US government conduct; the clearance is absence of adverse finding; the lift restores status quo. None generates a scored change.

The White House 30-day vetting framework for Fable 5 is the next forward factor — a safety-pledge framework that, if breached by Anthropic, would be scored against it under System (SYS) and Integrity (INT) dimensions. Conversely, a documented costly safety refusal under the framework would be upgrade support.

EU AI Act full applicability on August 2 — 32 days. General-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. xAI and OpenAI have not published compliance roadmaps.

Anthropic at 59.1 is 0.9 points below the Functional-to-Established boundary at 60.0. Eleventh consecutive boundary-proximity cycle. The EU AI Act compliance record on August 2 is the next meaningful observable event for the score trajectory.

methodologyNotes.name: Codified-Policy Attribution — When In-Force Law Codifies Already-Priced Practice
methodologyNotes.description: The July 1 cycle applies the codified-policy attribution rule for the first time in the July series. When a law enters force and both Amnesty International and Human Rights Watch characterize it as codifying existing documented practices — rather than introducing new categories of conduct — the in-force date does not independently generate a new score dock. The harm the law authorizes was already scored in prior cycles. The marginal new impact is limited to the incremental codification effect: for China's Ethnic Unity Law, that is approximately two quarter-steps (Equity and Empathy), yielding 1.5 composite points — below the 5-point threshold. The law is NOT pre-adjudication — it is in force. But 'in force' and 'new threshold-clearing harm' are different standards. A first Article 63 enforcement action constitutes genuinely new realized harm and would be scored immediately when documented. This ruling establishes the precedent: codification of existing practice = confirmed score + forward trigger logged; codification of new conduct = scored immediately on first enforcement.

methodologyNotes.name: First-Assessment Baseline — California Exemplary Confirmation
methodologyNotes.description: California's first formal benchmark assessment follows the baseline-establishment procedure: the canonical composite formula applies within-window evidence to produce a starting score. California's base score of 79.7 (from weighted dimensional raw scores) plus an integration premium of 8.0 (full consistent performance across all eight dimensions, near-zero inter-dimension variance) yields 87.7 of 100 — the Exemplary band. Three policy launches effective July 1 are positive within-window signals but none crosses the 5-point threshold from 87.7 independently. The homelessness caseload (161,548) is chronic-context, not new-window evidence. The LA protest-policing incident is an ACC watch flag, not an independently threshold-clearing event. This baseline is the authoritative starting point for all future California assessments. Watch flags are opened for ACC, EQU, and ACT to trigger verification in subsequent cycles.

methodologyNotes.name: Exogenous-Event Attribution — Anthropic Model Ban Arc Resolved
methodologyNotes.description: The Anthropic model ban/clearance/lift arc (June 12–30) is fully resolved as net-neutral under the exogenous-event attribution rule. Three steps: (1) June 12 Commerce Department ban on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 — US government action, not Anthropic harm, not scorable against Anthropic. (2) June 26–27 clearance — absence of adverse finding, not new positive own-conduct, not an upgrade basis. (3) June 30 full lift — status quo restored, still not new positive own-conduct, not an upgrade basis. The May 2026 SYS/INT uplift for Anthropic's safety posture is already priced in the 59.1 score. The arc opened and closed within the assessment window with net delta 0.0. This ruling confirms the exogenous-event test: a government action ON an entity generates no direct score movement for the entity when (a) no safety harm was attributed to the entity and (b) no positive own-conduct resulted from the government's reversal.

forwardTriggers.trigger: 14-day reassessment checkpoint — standard interval for distinguishing capacity failure from deliberate obstruction. Documented aid obstruction, diversion, or suppression of casualty data is the downgrade trigger from 18.0 of 100. Sustained effective transparent relief is the upgrade trigger. Action (ACT) and Accountability (ACC) watch flags open. Death toll 1,943; 43,200 missing.
Full resolution-phase reassessment due. Conversion trigger: reversal of the June 8 codified-impunity law and release of detained union leaders.

Q2 2026 earnings call — material DOJ disclosures, investor-facing admissions about probe scope, or settlement announcements are scored triggers under Accountability (ACC) and Integrity (INT) dimensions. DOJ probes have widened to Optum Rx physician reimbursement and MA-inflation billing.

July natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window — active. France and Germany imported cases confirmed; US evacuation underway. Documented government obstruction of WHO response corridors is the floor-crossing trigger toward 0 of 100. Contact tracing at approximately 45 percent; no approved vaccine.
EU AI Act full applicability — general-purpose AI transparency obligations, Article 50 labeling, and whistleblower protections activate. No public compliance roadmap from xAI as of this cycle.

EU AI Act full applicability — same obligations apply. US-government-mandated restricted GPT-5.6 release (June 29) is US government conduct, not OpenAI own-conduct; logged for next rotation. 42-state attorney general proceedings ongoing.

EU AI Act full applicability — 32 days away. Anthropic's compliance infrastructure is documented. White House 30-day vetting framework resolution is the next SYS/BND forward factor. Model ban arc resolved net-neutral June 30.

August IPC lean-season update — IPC Phase 5 Borno trajectory and government response adequacy. Actual WFP food-assistance cuts and a realized Phase 5 famine declaration would be scored triggers from the June 19 band crossing to 18.0 of 100.

Famine declaration window open through September 2026. Buur Hakaba and Baidoa both at IPC AMN Phase 5. End-of-June formal declaration threshold passed without issuance. A formal IPC Phase 5 famine declaration in Somalia combined with documented government aid obstruction is a scored change trigger for the 4.7-of-100 score.

First documented Article 63 enforcement action under the Ethnic Unity and Progress Law — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention of a diaspora member. Law entered force July 1; enforcement has not yet occurred. Any first enforcement action constitutes new realized harm scorable toward Equity (EQU) and Boundaries (BND) docks.

A realized Ebola incursion from DRC's Haut-Uele province into South Sudan, combined with documented failure of South Sudan's own emergency response, would constitute scorable own-conduct — the first cross-floor epidemiological trigger in the series. DRC's Haut-Uele borders South Sudan.

An implemented civilian-protecting ceasefire with unobstructed aid access is scored positive own-conduct. RSF commencement of a direct ground assault on El Obeid residential zones — following the El-Fasher pattern — is the next floor-reinforcement event. Al-Burhan's ceasefire rejection stands.

A new ceasefire agreement AND Lebanese Armed Forces assuming sovereign control of southern Lebanon AND reconstruction delivery are together the scored positive own-conduct trigger from 17.2 of 100. The June 28 Washington framework is closed. A new diplomatic process is required.

Documented discriminatory denial of emergency protection to stateless bidoon or migrant workers during wartime — Equity (EQU) downgrade trigger from 25.0 of 100. Durable and independently verified expansion of bidoon rights or citizenship recognition — EQU upgrade trigger.

Documented wartime crackdown, mass detention of Shia-majority population, or discriminatory denial of emergency protection — EQU and Accountability (ACC) downgrade trigger from 9.4 of 100. Genuine verified release of political prisoners or restoration of civil and political rights — upgrade trigger.

Durable reversal of Houthi-forced WFP staff terminations and release of all 73 detained UN workers would be scored positive own-conduct — the upgrade trigger from 0 of 100. Whether Doha talks produce a humanitarian corridor for Yemen is the observable forward question.

Coordinator-level Sahel-band calibration review — Mali (12.5 of 100) versus reweighted peer Burkina Faso (6.3 of 100) on comparable UN-documented state-perpetration conduct. Calibration flag in its eighth consecutive day; no timeline set.
42-state attorney general proceedings and Florida criminal investigation — no fixed hearing dates; any concluded finding, settlement, or criminal charge constitutes a scored change trigger.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: China's Ethnic Unity and Progress Law entered force July 1, codifying assimilation and activating Article 63 extraterritorial diaspora liability. Does a first documented enforcement action — prosecution, forced rendition, or family detention — constitute new threshold-clearing harm on the Equity dimension, or continuation of China's already-priced 19.5-of-100 Critical conduct pattern?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: The law is in force but codifies already-priced practice — the methodology distinguishes first enforcement from continuation.
